skill.name: theme-factory
skill.description: Apply curated color palettes and font pairings to slides, documents, reports, landing pages, and other visual artifacts.
skill.summary: Styles visual artifacts with curated or custom themes.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, theme vocabulary, and color names as possible command execution, weak cryptography, or C2 indicators. Manual review found these are false positives in prose and theme specifications, with no executable code, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown inline code spans for file names, color hex values, and theme references. No evidence found of executable commands, script files, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain markdown content only, and the repository has no executable implementation files in the scanned structure. This strongly supports a false-positive assessment.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Matches Are Benign Theme Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and C2 keyword detections map to ordinary design words, theme names, color names, and descriptions. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, command-and-control behavior, encoded payloads, or suspicious instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are static markdown descriptions and palette entries. Their surrounding context is visual design guidance, not security-sensitive logic.

content.user_title: Apply Polished Themes to Creative Artifacts
content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent styling across slides, reports, documents, and web artifacts. This skill provides curated palettes, font pairings, and a process for applying or creating themes.
actual_capabilities: Presents ten predefined themes with palette, typography, and usage guidance.
Helps users choose a theme before applying visual changes.
Applies selected colors and fonts consistently across an artifact.
Supports custom theme generation when preset themes do not fit.
Uses a showcase PDF and per-theme markdown files as references.
limitations: It provides design guidance, not a rendering engine or file converter.
It depends on the host assistant to edit the target artifact.
It does not verify brand compliance against external brand guidelines.
Some listed fonts may require local availability or substitution.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Presentation Deck
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent visual theme to an existing slide deck for clearer communication and stronger visual polish.

use_cases.title: Style a Report or Document
use_cases.description: Use a palette and font pairing to make a written artifact more readable, organized, and visually consistent.

use_cases.title: Create a Custom Visual Direction
use_cases.description: Generate a new theme from a project description when the preset choices do not match the audience or subject.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Preset Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for guided theme selection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the available themes for this artifact, then help me choose one that fits a professional audience.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Selected Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: User already knows which preset to use.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Ocean Depths theme to my presentation. Keep the content unchanged and improve consistency, contrast, and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Match Theme to Audience
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a recommendation based on context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this report and recommend the best preset theme for executives. Explain the choice briefly before applying it.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Custom Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a new theme direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom theme for a cybersecurity product launch. Define the palette, typography, best uses, and then apply it to the artifact.

output_examples.input: Apply a calm professional theme to a financial report.
output_examples.output: The report uses a navy, teal, seafoam, and cream palette with clean sans serif typography. Headings, callouts, and tables now follow one visual system.

output_examples.input: Create a warm theme for a restaurant pitch deck.
output_examples.output: The deck receives a warm palette with mustard, terracotta, beige, and dark brown accents. Section headers, backgrounds, and emphasis styles match the hospitality tone.

output_examples.input: Help me choose a theme for an AI startup launch page.
output_examples.output: The recommended direction is Tech Innovation because it uses high contrast and bright accents. It fits software launches and digital transformation content.

best_practices: Review the theme showcase before selecting a preset theme.
Confirm the target audience and artifact type before applying styles.
Check contrast, font availability, and visual consistency after styling.
anti_patterns: Do not apply a theme before the user confirms the choice.
Do not change core content while performing a styling pass.
Do not combine multiple preset themes without a clear design reason.

faq.question: What artifacts can this skill style?
faq.answer: It can guide styling for slides, documents, reports, landing pages, and similar visual artifacts.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes ten predefined themes with color palettes, font pairings, and suggested use cases.

faq.question: Can it create a new theme?
faq.answer: Yes. It can generate a custom theme from a project description when presets are not suitable.

faq.question: Does it automatically edit files?
faq.answer: No. It provides theme guidance that the host assistant can apply using available editing tools.

faq.question: Are the themes brand-specific?
faq.answer: No. They are general visual directions and should be adapted for formal brand standards.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found only static markdown design guidance and no executable or malicious behavior.
